title: Files, Directories, Links, and Metadata · Terminal Failure Triage
summary: Prepare a service workspace while preserving names, metadata, link intent, and the ability to reverse every operation. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reversible filesystem-change plan and evidence tree and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
Predict inode, path, deletion, filesystem, and permission behavior for hard and symbolic links.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Safe Globbing evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Safe Globbing assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Workspace contains README.md, data/incidents.csv, scripts/check.sh, and symbolic link current → data. Add reports/run-4.txt, preserve metadata, preview wildcard scope, and prove removal cannot escape /home/learner/lab. Competency probe: Preview wildcard expansion and protect leading-hyphen, whitespace, newline, empty-match, and hidden-name cases.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Safe Globbing has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Safe Globbing precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Workspace contains README.md, data/incidents.csv, scripts/check.sh, and symbolic link current → data. Add reports/run-4.txt, preserve metadata, preview wildcard scope, and prove removal cannot escape /home/learner/lab. Competency probe: Preview wildcard expansion and protect leading-hyphen, whitespace, newline, empty-match, and hidden-name cases.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Quoting a wildcard still expands it safely.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior for the debug Safe Globbing case
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary for the debug Safe Globbing case
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output for the debug Safe Globbing case
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff for the debug Safe Globbing case

steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-safe-globbing-1nylam" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Globbing into observable performance evidence.
